cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about what Steve Rowson thinks of the newer football helmets. The options are A to D. First, I need to find the part of the text where Steve Rowson is mentioned. The text says: "Steve Rowson is an engineer at Virginia Tech. In his lab, he tests how helmets hold up against different types of hits. No helmet can prevent concussions completely, says Rowson. But he's found that when wearing a newer football helmet, the player's head doesn't gain speed as much after a hit. That lowers the risk of concussions." So, according to this, Rowson says that newer helmets make the player's head not gain speed as much, reducing concussion risk. The key point here is that the newer helmets are better at reducing the speed gain, which in turn lowers concussion risk. That implies that the newer helmets are more effective than older ones. Looking at the options: (B) They work better than the old ones. That seems correct because the text states that newer helmets reduce the risk compared to not having them. Also, the fact that he's an engineer testing helmets and stating that the newer ones lower the risk directly supports option B. The other options: A says they vary in size, but the text doesn't mention size variations. C says they slow down the player's running speed. The text says
